retrieve_facts.procedure_description: ACDF C5-C6, one level (anterior cervical discectomy with interbody fusion, single level)

extract_facts.denial_notice_text:
PACIFIC WESTERN HEALTH PLAN — NOTICE OF NON-COVERAGE DETERMINATION

Date: April 8, 2026
Member: Margaret Williams
Member ID: M-4471829
Provider: Dr. Elena Rodriguez, Pacific Coast Orthopedics
Requested Service: Anterior cervical discectomy and fusion at C5-C6 (CPT 22551)

DETERMINATION: Service not authorized

CLINICAL CRITERIA APPLIED:
This determination was made by applying the criteria set forth in CC-SPINE-2024 (Plan Medical Policy: Cervical Spine Surgery), specifically:
  - § 2.1 (Diagnosis requirement: cervical radiculopathy, myelopathy, or disc herniation with neurological deficit)
  - § 2.2 (Conservative treatment requirement: minimum 6 weeks supervised physical therapy plus failed pharmacotherapy with at least two agent classes plus interventional procedure trial or documented waiver)

CLINICAL REASONING:
The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the criteria are met. Specifically:

  Regarding § 2.1 (Diagnosis): The clinical record describes chronic axial neck pain with bilateral shoulder girdle involvement. The neurological examination on March 18, 2026 documents intact strength (5/5 throughout bilateral upper extremities), preserved reflexes (2+ biceps, triceps, brachioradialis bilaterally), and intact sensation without dermatomal distribution. Spurling's maneuver was negative bilaterally. No motor or sensory radicular signs are documented. The presenting clinical picture is consistent with axial cervical pain without radiculopathy, which does not satisfy § 2.1.

  Regarding § 2.2 (Conservative treatment): The submitted records document one course of physical therapy of approximately 4 weeks duration in August-September 2025, with patient discontinuation noted prior to discharge. This is below the 6-week minimum specified in § 2.2(a). Pharmacotherapy is documented as intermittent over-the-counter ibuprofen use; no trial of additional agent classes (gabapentin, tricyclics, muscle relaxants, or other) is documented, which does not satisfy § 2.2(b). No interventional procedure (epidural steroid injection, facet injection, or medial branch block) has been attempted or documented as contraindicated, which does not satisfy § 2.2(c).

The imaging evaluation (cervical MRI dated January 14, 2026) documents mild multilevel degenerative changes with no significant central canal or foraminal stenosis and no nerve root impingement. The imaging does not demonstrate the structural pathology contemplated by § 2.3.

Alternatives covered under the member's benefits include continued conservative management including a structured 6-week course of supervised physical therapy, trial of additional pharmacological agents per § 2.2(b), and interventional pain management consultation.

RIGHT TO INDEPENDENT MEDICAL REVIEW:
Under California Health & Safety Code § 1374.30 and § 1374.31, you have the right to request an Independent Medical Review (IMR) of this determination. The IMR is conducted by physicians not affiliated with the plan. You may request an IMR by contacting the California Department of Managed Health Care at 1-888-466-2219 or by submitting an application at www.healthhelp.ca.gov. The IMR application must be filed within 6 months of this notice. If you have any questions about the IMR process or this determination, please contact Member Services at 1-800-555-0119.

This decision constitutes a non-coverage determination subject to internal appeal and IMR rights as described above. You also have the right to internal grievance under the plan's Grievance Procedure.

Reviewer: Dr. Thomas Andrews, MD, Medical Director
Date of review: April 8, 2026

End of Notice.
extract_facts.primary_diagnosis: Chronic axial cervical pain with bilateral shoulder girdle pain. Mild multilevel cervical spondylosis (C4-C5, C5-C6, C6-C7 disc desiccation). No radiculopathy documented. No myelopathy documented. No disc herniation with neurological deficit documented.
extract_facts.presenting_diagnosis: Chronic cervical pain (M54.2), bilateral shoulder pain (M25.519). Cervical spondylosis without myelopathy (M47.812). Patient seeking surgical evaluation.
extract_facts.imaging_findings: MRI cervical spine, 2026-01-14: Mild multilevel cervical spondylosis. C4-C5: mild disc desiccation, small posterior bulge, no significant central or foraminal stenosis. C5-C6: moderate disc desiccation, small broad-based posterior disc bulge measuring 1.5mm, no central stenosis, mild bilateral neural foraminal narrowing without nerve root impingement. C6-C7: mild disc desiccation, no significant bulge. No evidence of disc herniation, no central canal stenosis, no severe foraminal stenosis, no nerve root impingement, no cord compression, no myelomalacia signal. Impression: Mild multilevel cervical degenerative changes without significant neural compromise.

imaging_reports.report_text: FINDINGS: Vertebral body heights and alignment are maintained. There is mild multilevel disc desiccation most prominent at C5-C6 with a small broad-based posterior disc bulge measuring approximately 1.5 mm. No focal disc protrusion or herniation. Central canal is patent throughout. Bilateral C5-C6 neural foramina show mild narrowing without definite nerve root impingement. C4-C5 and C6-C7 show mild disc desiccation only. Spinal cord signal is normal throughout the cervical spine without evidence of myelomalacia or cord compression. Paraspinal soft tissues are unremarkable. IMPRESSION: Mild multilevel cervical degenerative changes without significant central canal or foraminal stenosis. No evidence of disc herniation or nerve root impingement. No findings of myelomalacia or cord compression.

extract_facts.serial_imaging_comparison: No prior cervical imaging available for comparison.
extract_facts.neurological_exam_findings: Examination dated 2026-03-18 by Dr. Elena Rodriguez: Mental status intact. Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Motor: strength 5/5 throughout bilateral upper extremities including deltoid, biceps, triceps, wrist extension, finger flexion, intrinsic hand muscles. Sensation: intact to light touch, pinprick, and proprioception throughout C4-T1 distributions bilaterally. No dermatomal sensory loss documented. Reflexes: biceps 2+ bilaterally, triceps 2+ bilaterally, brachioradialis 2+ bilaterally. No Hoffmann sign. No clonus. Spurling's test negative bilaterally. Shoulder abduction relief sign negative. Range of motion: cervical flexion 50°, extension 40°, lateral rotation 70° bilaterally, lateral flexion 35° bilaterally with end-range discomfort. Tenderness to palpation over bilateral paraspinal muscles C5-T1 level and bilateral trapezii. Adson's maneuver negative.
extract_facts.neurological_exam_notes: No focal neurological deficit. No evidence of radiculopathy. No evidence of myelopathy. Examination consistent with axial cervical and shoulder girdle myofascial pain.
extract_facts.functional_status: Patient reports neck and shoulder pain rated 5-6/10 baseline, 7-8/10 with prolonged sitting or computer work. Able to perform all activities of daily living independently. Continues working as administrative assistant 40 hours per week. Reports decreased tolerance for prolonged keyboard work and occasional sleep disruption due to discomfort.
extract_facts.functional_outcome_measures: Neck Disability Index score: 24/50 (moderate disability). No formal pre-/post-treatment measurement comparison available.
extract_facts.symptoms_documented: Bilateral cervical paraspinal pain, bilateral trapezius and shoulder girdle pain, intermittent suboccipital headaches. No upper extremity radiation in dermatomal distribution. No numbness. No weakness. No gait disturbance. No bowel or bladder symptoms.
extract_facts.clinical_guidelines_applicability: AANS/CNS 2023 Cervical Spine Surgery Guidelines: ACDF is appropriate first-line consideration for cervical radiculopathy with imaging-correlated nerve root compression after failed conservative treatment, or for cervical myelopathy. The guidelines do not endorse ACDF for chronic axial neck pain without radiculopathy or myelopathy (TIER_3). NASS 2020 Clinical Guidelines on Cervical Spine Disorders: Surgical intervention for axial neck pain without specific structural pathology is not supported by current evidence. The patient's clinical presentation does not match the indications described in either guideline source.
extract_facts.pt_records: Physical therapy at Westside Rehabilitation, August 5, 2025 through September 2, 2025 (4 weeks, 8 sessions). Initial evaluation documented neck and bilateral shoulder pain, decreased cervical range of motion, and bilateral trapezius muscle tension. Treatment included therapeutic exercise, manual therapy, postural training, and home exercise instruction. Patient showed improvement in cervical range of motion and reported decreased pain (initial 7/10 to discharge 4/10) over the course of treatment. The patient self-discontinued treatment on September 2, 2025, citing 'feeling better and needing to return to full work schedule.' The course of treatment was 4 weeks, below the 6-week minimum specified in plan policy § 2.2(a).
extract_facts.pt_discharge_summary: Discharge summary, Westside Rehabilitation, 2025-09-02: Patient discontinued before completing planned 6-week course. Final session pain rating 4/10 (initial 7/10). Cervical range of motion improved by approximately 15% bilaterally. Patient declined further sessions. Discharge status: improvement noted, plan of care not completed.
extract_facts.pt_progress_notes: Progress notes consistent with discharge summary. No documentation of plateau, contraindication, or futility. Treating PT documented continued expected progress at time of discontinuation. Notes contain language: 'Patient making expected gains and would benefit from completion of planned care; however, patient elects to discontinue.'
extract_facts.pharmacotherapy_records: Pharmacy fill history (Pacific Western pharmacy benefit, 2024-2026): Ibuprofen 800mg tablets, intermittent fills approximately every 2-3 months at #30 per fill. No fills for gabapentin, pregabalin, tricyclic antidepressants, muscle relaxants (cyclobenzaprine, methocarbamol, or other), tramadol, or opioid analgesics. Patient reports 'over-the-counter ibuprofen as needed' in clinical history. No documented trial of agent classes beyond NSAIDs.
extract_facts.interventional_records: No interventional procedures documented. No cervical epidural steroid injection. No facet injection. No medial branch block. No documentation of interventional procedure being contraindicated.
extract_facts.conservative_treatment_history: Conservative treatment history summary: (1) Four weeks of supervised physical therapy August-September 2025, self-discontinued after improvement (below 6-week plan minimum, no documented contraindication to completion). (2) Intermittent NSAID use (ibuprofen) without trial of additional agent classes. (3) No interventional procedures attempted or documented as contraindicated. (4) No alternative treatment approaches documented (e.g., chiropractic, acupuncture, behavioral pain management).
extract_facts.treating_physician_documentation: Treating physician: Dr. Elena Rodriguez, orthopedic surgery, Pacific Coast Orthopedics. Documentation includes (1) initial consultation 2026-02-10 with H&P, (2) follow-up 2026-03-18 with discussion of imaging and treatment options, (3) pre-authorization request submitted 2026-03-25 citing 'chronic cervical pain refractory to conservative measures.' Physician documentation does not include explicit attestation that conservative treatment was completed (it was not — 4 weeks of PT rather than 6, no pharmacotherapy trial beyond NSAIDs, no interventional procedures). The clinical rationale section describes the patient's chronic pain and functional limitation but does not describe imaging-correlated nerve root pathology or neurological deficit. Functional limitations are described in general terms (decreased tolerance for prolonged sitting, occasional sleep disruption) without formal functional capacity evaluation. Surgical risks and alternatives are noted in passing in the consultation note ('discussed risks of surgery including infection, hardware failure, adjacent segment disease, and alternatives including continued conservative care') without detailed informed consent documentation in the pre-auth submission. The member appeal letter dated 2026-04-22 requests reconsideration on grounds that 'conservative treatment has been tried and has not provided lasting relief.'
extract_facts.member_appeal_letter: Member appeal dated 2026-04-22 from Margaret Williams. Letter requests reconsideration of the denial. States that the patient has been suffering with neck and shoulder pain for over a year, that physical therapy 'helped temporarily but the pain returned,' and that NSAIDs 'don't work well enough.' Letter does not provide additional clinical documentation. Does not assert procedural defect in the denial. Does not cite specific regulatory provisions. Requests that the plan reconsider 'to allow surgery to address my chronic pain.'
extract_facts.appeal_grounds_asserted: Member asserts: (1) chronic pain duration justifies surgical consideration; (2) conservative treatments tried have not provided lasting relief. Member does not assert: factual errors in the denial, procedural defects, regulatory carve-out applicability, or specific plan criterion satisfaction.
